1. The author is dealt two cards from a 52-card deck. To calculate the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards, the number of favorable combinations is calculated using combinations. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from 52 is C(52, 5). The number of unfavorable combinations (no hearts) is C(39, 5). The probability is 1 - [C(39, 5)/C(52, 5)]. This simplifies to a closed form expression.

The author should choose Strategy B as it has a higher expected value.
